# RelayPlay

> Pure TypeScript framework for local-first web games with server-relayed
> progress and occasional canonical interactions. No P2P.

Primary docs:
- README.md: product overview and quick start
- docs/requirements.md: authoritative capabilities and non-goals
- docs/architecture.md: package and data-flow boundaries
- docs/configuration.md: flags, presets, and recipes
- docs/protocol.md: message classes, ordering, resume, and scheduling
- docs/security.md: trust model, authentication hooks, limits, ranked caveats
- docs/game-design.md: mobile/desktop, Tetris-like, and rhythm-like guidance
- docs/deploy-cloudflare.md: deployment and operations
- docs/verification.md: requirement-to-code-to-test traceability
- docs/ai-guide.md: deterministic coding-agent workflow
- relayplay.config.schema.json: machine-readable configuration contract
- AGENTS.md: repository invariants and validation commands

Packages:
- packages/core: config + protocol + validators + time math
- packages/client: browser SDK + reconnect + cadence + capability helpers
- packages/server: provider-neutral room engine + in-memory adapter
- packages/cloudflare: Worker + Durable Object adapter
- examples/live-race: complete browser example

Hard rules:
- Never implement WebRTC/P2P.
- Never block the local game loop on network response.
- Treat progress as replaceable telemetry and canonical interactions as an
  append-only, persist-before-broadcast log.
- Schedule remote gameplay effects in the future at a logical boundary.
- Runtime-validate all wire messages.
- Client reports are not authoritative ranked evidence without a verifier.

Validation:
`npm run verify`
